articles.title: China's chatbot industry is fiercely competing for customers. Cue the freebies
articles.summary: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

articles.title: How a SCOTUS decision on birthright citizenship could impact education access
articles.summary: All children, regardless of immigration status, have the right to a free K-12 public education. But without birthright citizenship, access to schools and colleges could get complicated.

articles.title: TSA workers may receive pay soon. And, Israel plans to expand its invasion of Lebanon
articles.summary: TSA workers have now been without pay for more than 40 days, as Trump says he has a plan to pay them. And, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announces plans to expand the invasion of Lebanon.

articles.title: Trump weighing all options on Iran's Kharg Island
articles.summary: The president said the U.S. could "take the oil in Iran" and that he was considering sending U.S. forces to seize Kharg Island's oil terminal.

articles.title: War Pushes Gas Prices Near $4 a Gallon, and Anti-Trump Protesters Rally Nationwide
articles.summary: Plus, a surge of Catholic converts in the United States.

articles.title: Philanthropy in science has little oversight. Jeffrey Epstein exploited that
articles.summary: A large share of science funding comes through philanthropy, with little legal or public scrutiny. This lack of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to cultivate scientists and launder his reputation.

articles.title: Trump administration cuts turned rural towns into sitting ducks for disasters
articles.summary: The Trump administration has delayed billions of dollars for projects to protect Americans from floods, wildfires and hurricanes. Local leaders are increasingly anxious.

articles.title: What We Know About the T.S.A. and ICE Presence at Airports
articles.summary: Transportation Security Agency officers have called out of work or quit after weeks of not getting paid during a partial government shutdown. ICE agents have begun to fill their roles.

articles.title: A New Boss Takes Over Homeland Security With Less Flash but Same Mission
articles.summary: Markwayne Mullin, the incoming D.H.S. secretary, faces a difficult balance: a public reset of the agency while delivering on President Trump’s deportation agenda.

articles.title: Private-Credit Wobbles Could Prove Perilous for Trump
articles.summary: The Trump administration is poised to broaden access to risky investments that are showing signs of strain.

articles.title: Texas Border Wall Plan in Big Bend Draws Opposition From Republicans and Democrats
articles.summary: Plans for a wall in Big Bend National Park prompted public protests and behind-the-scenes lobbying to sway the Trump administration to stop.

articles.title: How much protein do you need? Here's how to personalize your optimal intake
articles.summary: The Dietary Guidelines released this year recommend higher levels of this essential nutrient. But protein needs are personal. Here's how to assess yours.

articles.title: As birthright citizenship goes to Supreme Court, here's how Americans feel about it
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear arguments Wednesday on whether all children born in the United States can continue to automatically receive citizenship.

articles.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
articles.summary: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

articles.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
articles.summary: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

articles.title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
articles.summary: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.

articles.title: U.S. Allows Russian Oil Tanker to Reach Cuba, Despite Blockade
articles.summary: The tanker full of crude oil could reach its expected destination by Monday, providing a lifeline to the island amid intense U.S. pressure.

articles.title: The rippling effect of the Iran war, seen from the border with Turkey
articles.summary: U.S. marines have arrived in the Middle East. NPR's Emily Feng has been monitoring the latest developments from the border of Iran and Turkey.

articles.title: New Political Group to Push Trump’s A.I. Agenda in Midterms
articles.summary: The group, Innovation Council Action, says it plans to spend at least $100 million. It will be led by a former administration official.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

articles.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
articles.summary: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

articles.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
articles.summary: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
